description: A long thin strand of cotton, silk, or other fibers used in sewing or weaving. 'Sima' names the single strand—fiber spun into continuous line.
sound_symbolism: The word spins fine. 'Si' draws out thin and precise, fiber extending. Then 'ma' grounds in material substance, thread ready for use.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo sima' (the thread). The basic unit of textile—becomes 'wetha' (cloth) through weaving.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Spinning thread—the ancient skill of drawing fiber into continuous strand, foundation of all textile.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Connection as thread—relationships woven strand by strand into fabric of community.
